articles.title: A 'Living Fossil' With a Twisted Jaw: This 275-Million-Year-Old Tetrapod Defies Explanation
articles.summary: Paleontologists in Brazil unearthed nine fossil jaws from Tanyka amnicola, a stem tetrapod with teeth pointing sideways. The 275-million-year-old herbivore was a "living fossil" even in its time, like a platypus among mammals. Its bizarre jaw hints at a unique grinding feeding style.

articles.title: AI Outperforms Doctors at Diagnostic Reasoning, but Experts Urge Caution
articles.summary: OpenAI's o1-preview model correctly diagnosed challenging medical cases nearly 80% of the time, surpassing human clinicians. In one instance, it flagged a flesh-eating infection 12 hours before doctors. The study in Science reignites debate on AI's role in medicine.

articles.title: Rethinking Cybersecurity: Why AI Must Be at the Core, Not an Afterthought
articles.summary: At MIT Technology Review's EmTech AI conference, cybersecurity expert Tarique Mustafa argued that legacy security systems can't handle AI-driven threats. With AI expanding the attack surface, security must be rebuilt with autonomous AI at its foundation—not layered on top.
